content.text: Let me inspect the current state before making changes.

input.command:
set -x
NS=karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-lifec-447dced7-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS get pods
echo "--- users ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_users
echo "--- perms /app ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app
echo "--- perms /ops ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /ops
echo "--- app-client logs ---"
kubectl -n $NS logs deploy/app-client --tail=20
echo "--- ops-client logs ---"
kubectl -n $NS logs deploy/ops-client --tail=20

content.text: Found the issues: `app-user` has empty `configure` on `/app`, `ops-user` has full but *unauthorized* access to `/app`, and `ops-user` has no permissions at all on `/ops`. Fixing all three.

input.command:
set -x
NS=karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-lifec-447dced7-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl clear_permissions -p /app ops-user
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /ops ops-user ".*" ".*" ".*"
echo "--- perms /app ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /app
echo "--- perms /ops ---"
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_permissions -p /ops

content.text: Permissions are now correctly scoped. Let's also confirm `app-user` has no leftover permissions on `/ops`, then restart the crashing client pods.

input.command:
set -x
NS=karma-rabbitmq-security-upgrade-lifec-447dced7-cluster-a
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_user_permissions app-user
kubectl -n $NS exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl list_user_permissions ops-user
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=app-client
kubectl -n $NS delete pod -l app=ops-client
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/app-client --timeout=90s
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deploy/ops-client --timeout=90s
